decision: Stop; investigate transient store, lock, or refresh availability before any login.
nextStep: Bind the exact target and reconcile principal, connector, installation, profile, prefix, cipher, deployment, and environment; preserve the newest fenced generation and obtain separate mutation authority.
evidenceRequired: Exact target error and authenticated Vercel and Upstash metadata.
Same-subject stored-profile proof and fully reconciled identity/store/cipher fields.
Mutation approval only after permanent-failure classification.
limitations: A temporary auth failure does not establish a need for reauthentication.
nonClaims: Local stored-profile proof does not prove a later provider token or Production request.
rollbackRevocation: Quarantine and preserve the newest fenced generation; do not restore an older generation or edit Upstash. The provider account owner controls token/session revocation.
unsafeAmbiguities: Differing login and proxy defaults are an intentional hard stop, not a fallback.
